channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Coal mine gas explosion in China kills 82 people, state media say
item.description: A gas explosion at a coal mine in China's northern province of Shanxi killed at least 82 people. Official news agency Xinhua said the accident happened on Friday evening and 247 workers were trapped.
item.content:encoded:
A gas explosion at a coal mine in China's northern province of Shanxi killed at least 82 people. Official news agency Xinhua said the accident happened on Friday evening and 247 workers were trapped.

(Image credit: Cao Yang)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump administration to force foreigners in the U.S. to apply for a green card abroad
item.description: Foreigners in the U.S. who want a green card will need to leave and apply in their home country, the Trump administration announced Friday, in a surprise change to a longstanding policy.
item.content:encoded:
Foreigners in the U.S. who want a green card will need to leave and apply in their home country, the Trump administration announced Friday, in a surprise change to a longstanding policy.

(Image credit: Wilfredo Lee)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Rubio arrives in India ahead of Quad talks as U.S. tries to reset strained ties
item.description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio arrived in India on Saturday ahead of a meeting next week with his counterparts from India, Australia, and Japan, members of the Indo-Pacific strategic alliance.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio arrived in India on Saturday ahead of a meeting next week with his counterparts from India, Australia, and Japan, members of the Indo-Pacific strategic alliance.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Hunger grows in Gaza as food aid is cut due to higher costs caused by war in Iran
item.description: The U.S.-Israeli war against Iran is driving up the cost of food, leading to funding shortfalls and higher expenses for critical food aid in Gaza.
item.content:encoded: The U.S.-Israeli war against Iran is driving up the cost of food, leading to funding shortfalls and higher expenses for critical food aid in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Anas Baba

item.title: The aftermath of Trump-Xi summit: comparing U.S. and China announcements
item.description: Analysts say a comparison of the readouts issued by the U.S. and China reveals "minor inconsistencies" on issues such as agriculture, tariffs and rare earths. But, experts argue, those differences are not significant.
item.content:encoded:
Analysts say a comparison of the readouts issued by the U.S. and China reveals "minor inconsistencies" on issues such as agriculture, tariffs and rare earths. But, experts argue, those differences are not significant.

(Image credit: GREG BAKER)
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Pak

item.title: Italy's prime minister wants Israel to apologize for treatment of flotilla activists
item.description: Italy's Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni wants an apology from Israel over the treatment of Italian citizens on a flotilla bound for Gaza.
item.content:encoded: Italy's Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni wants an apology from Israel over the treatment of Italian citizens on a flotilla bound for Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Megan Williams

item.title: UN gravely concerned by an Afghan Taliban law that has provisions on child marriage
item.description: The United Nations expressed "grave concern" on Thursday about a new law issued by Afghanistan's Taliban government on separation in marriage which includes provisions on child marriage.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations expressed "grave concern" on Thursday about a new law issued by Afghanistan's Taliban government on separation in marriage which includes provisions on child marriage.

(Image credit: Dar Yasin)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump says he's sending 5,000 more troops to Poland
item.description: President Trump's announcement stirred confusion in Europe following weeks of changing statements from his administration about reducing the American military footprint in Europe.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump's announcement stirred confusion in Europe following weeks of changing statements from his administration about reducing the American military footprint in Europe.

(Image credit: Jacquelyn Martin)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Rubio doubtful of diplomacy with Cuba as Trump raises new threat of military action
item.description: President Trump and America's top diplomat on Thursday again raised the specter of U.S. military intervention in Cuba, a day after the administration announced criminal charges against the island's former leader, Raúl Castro.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump and America's top diplomat on Thursday again raised the specter of U.S. military intervention in Cuba, a day after the administration announced criminal charges against the island's former leader, Raúl Castro.

(Image credit: Stefano Rellandini)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump's Raúl Castro indictment sends a signal to Cuba
item.description: The indictment of Raul Castro is the latest move by the Trump administration to ratchet up pressure on the Cuban government.
item.content:encoded: The indictment of Raul Castro is the latest move by the Trump administration to ratchet up pressure on the Cuban government.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas
